ops.text:
PHASE F DONE — SHIPPED 1.0.0 @ 3e2b6044 (tag v1.0.0) + 1.0.1 @ 74b587c0 (tag v1.0.1), both release runs green after fixes; plugin cache updated to 1.0.1 at user scope (name gotcha: `claude plugin update codex@skills` — the marketplace is "skills", bare `codex` and `codex@cc-skills` both "not found").

Design review (opus xhigh): NO blockers; 1 MAJOR fixed pre-tag (codex-wrapper-async had no instruction for an await-tool ERROR — dead daemon — vs the benign "no directive" notice; now falls back to disk truth via the AWAIT: line) + 4 prose minors applied (affirmative greeting detection, one-dispatch-per-owner rule, status-first re-park in SKILL.md, workflow-clause clarification); RelayStep-in-instructions kept (parents need it), substrate header cosmetic skipped, --version CI smoke deferred as low-priority.

Ship-prep landed @ b2ec70bf: goreleaser -X main.appVersion (snapshot verified 0.0.0-SNAPSHOT-*), --version flag, appVersion default "dev" (installer dev-guard), pinned-mode install-binary.sh as cc-guides fragment (derived from the pack template via sed after the comments guard blocked a hand transcription — papercut filed) + SessionStart hook + bin/ gitignored, capt-hook pack test + CGO_ENABLED=0 build-parity gate in codex-ci.yml. The parity gate exists because goreleaser builds nocgo and cgo-only CI let an incompatible dep pairing through silently — discovered when the user's concurrent daemonkit-v1 work (daemonrole/LifecycleBuild in consumer.go + go.mod bump) briefly made cc-interact v0.12.0 uncompilable nocgo; their b8faccf9 repin (cc-interact v0.14.1-0.20260721014621) settled it. Substrate coordination cost two ship refusals (foreign writing-docs-rewrite bookmark → --bookmark main; cc-guides.lock 2-sided rebase conflict → newest pin).

Release incidents, both fixed forward: (1) v1.0.0 run failed the cask push — cc-skills had NO HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN (first binary release from this repo); set from 1Password (op://OpenClaw/HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN/credential piped straight to gh secret set), rerun green. (2) THE LIVE MATRIX CAUGHT A REAL DEFECT the goldens could not: the symlink-provisioned binary resolved PLUGIN_ROOT through EvalSymlinks into the Caskroom (no AGENTS.md) — every dispatch through the provisioned layout fail-closed. v1.0.1: readAgentsMd tries the unresolved invocation path first; TestDispatchThroughSymlinkFindsAgentsMd pins the layout.

CODEX QUOTA IS BACK (early vs the ~07-26 estimate): three real pong round trips — blocking via the cache symlink, async --dispatch with Monitor + --watch pinging the chat the settle record (matrix item 2 live), and the daemon lazy-autostarted at the daemonrole path (~/.cc-codex-ask/bin/codex-ask, item 6). Synthetic --owner from this session enqueued nothing (no subject — this session's pre-plane pack; in-contract per M3, greeting-gated). Matrix 3-5 need a fresh session (channel mounts at session start): cc-notes task 7e1146ad with criteria. Decision note superseded: 2660990b → c3304c23. Quota-outage memory deleted.
